THE PURPOSE OF THIS TASK ORDER IS TO PROVIDE DEVELOPMENT, TECHNICAL, ANALYTICAL, PLANNING, AND SYSTEM ENGINEERING SUPPORT FOR THE FWC IN EXECUTING JFFT RESPONSIBILITIES.

subaward_description: THE DIRECTOR OF NATIONAL INTELLIGENCE (DNI) HAS MANDATED THAT ALL INTELLIGENCE COMMUNITY (IC) MEMBERS TAKE ADVANTAGE OF THE IC INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY ENVIRONMENT BY MOVING TO COMMON PLATFORMS TO ENABLE THE SECURE SHARING OF INFORMATION AND THE ADOPTION OF INNOVATIVE TECHNOLOGIES. AS PART OF THIS LARGER EFFORT, DHS I&A WILL PROVIDE CLOUD PLATFORM ACCESS FOR MULTIPLE DHS INTELLIGENCE ENTERPRISE (IE) COMPONENTS AS WELL AS CLOUD ENGINEERING SERVICES TO FACILITATE THE ARCHITECTING, ENGINEERING AND IMPLEMENTATION OF SOLUTIONS THAT SUPPORT THE IE AND IC. DHS I&A WILL USE COMMERCIAL CLOUD SERVICES (C2S), AN AMAZON WEB SERVICES (AWS) CLOUD THAT IS AVAILABLE AT THE TOP SECRET, SECRET AND UNCLASSIFIED LEVELS. THE DEVSECOPS OPERATIONAL PROTOTYPE TASK WILL DEVELOP, TEST, AND DEPLOY AN AUTOMATED DEVSECOPS PIPELINE ON THE UNCLASSIFIED AWS AND C2S CLOUDS THAT PERMITS RAPID AGILE SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT ON THE UNCLASSIFIED SIDE AND DELIVERY INTO C2S.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: FUTURE SURFACE COMBATANT CONTROLS SUPPORT
subaward_description: THE CONTRACTOR SHALL PROVIDE CONTROL AND INFORMATION SYSTEM COMPUTER PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT FOR HULL, MECHANICAL, AND ELECTRICAL (HM&E) DEVELOPMENT, SECURITY AND OPERATIONS (DEVSECOPS) TOOLS AND INFRASTRUCTURE IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE SYSTEMS ENGINEERING PROCESS (SEP). THIS SUPPORT INCLUDES DEVELOPING C++, JAVA, AND PYTHON CODE FOR CONTROLS ALGORITHMS AND GRAPHICAL USER INTERFACES. THIS SUPPORT ALSO INCLUDES DEVELOPMENT WORK WITH KUBERNETES, ANSIBLE, TERRAFORM, JENKINS, AMAZON WEB SERVICES, POSTGRESQL AND OTHER SQL DATABASES, AND WORK ON EMBEDDED PLATFORMS. THE CONTRACTOR WILL ALSO SUPPORT MANAGEMENT OF LARGE SYSTEM LOG DATA SETS AND FACILITATE INTEROPERABILITY AND INTEGRATION OF OTHER CONTROL AND MACHINERY PLANT SYSTEMS.

subaward_description: SUPPLIER WILL PROVIDE CLIENT WITH ENGINEERING PERSONNEL TO SUPPORT THE USAF CLOUD ONE PROGRAM FOR THE PURPOSES OF, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, ARCHITECUTURAL ANALYSIS CONSULTATION, CLOUD-COMPUTING DESIGN AND IMPLEMENTATION, AND SOLUTION MIGRATION OF USAF APPLICATION FROM ON-PREMISE DATA CENTERS TO GOVERNMENT CLOUD ENVIRONMENTS PROVIDED BY THE CLOUD ONE PROGRAM (I.E. AMAZON WEB SERVICES, MICROSOFT AZURE, ORACLE CLOUD INFRASTRUCTURE, GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM). ENGINEERING PERSONNEL WILL BE ASSIGNED TEAMS AND TASKED BY CLIENT PROGRAM MANAGERS AND TECHNICAL LEADS AS A PART OF THE OVERALL GROUP OF CLOUD ONE MIGRATION, CYBER, O&S, OR DATA TRANSPORT TEAMS. WHILE TRAVEL IS EXPECTED TO BE LIMITED, TACTWARE WILL PROVIDE ON-SITE SUPPORT FOR CLIENT AND USAF NEED TASKED BY CLIENT PROGRAM MANAGERS AND TECHNICAL LEADS AS A PART OF THE OVERALL GROUP OF CLOUD ONE MIGRATION, CYBER, O&S, OR DATA TRANSPORT TEAMS. WHILE TRAVEL IS EXPECTED TO BE LIMITED, TACTWARE WILL PROVIDE ON-SITE SUPPORT FOR CLIENT AND USAF NEED TASKED BY CLIENT PROGRAM MANAGERS AND TECHNICAL LEADS AS A PART OF THE OVERALL GROUP OF CLOUD ONE MIGRATION, CYBER, O&S, OR DATA TRANSPORT TEAMS. WHILE TRAVEL IS EXPECTED TO BE LIMITED, TACTWARE WILL PROVIDE ON-SITE SUPPORT FOR CLIENT AND USAF NEEDS IN SUPPORT OF THE CLOUD ONE PROGRAM.

subaward_description:
BACKGROUND AND SCOPE
THE F-22 RAPTOR SYSTEMS PROGRAM OFFICE (SPO) IS PROTOTYPING AND DEVELOPING A CLOUD-BASED SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT ENVIRONMENT WITH IN THE AMAZON WEB SERVICES (AWS) SECRET CLOUD COMPUTE SERVICE (SC2S) REGION. THE VISION OF THE SPO IS TO MAXIMIZE EFFICIENCY AND AGILITY IN SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT TO MAINTAIN THE AIR SUPERIORITY OF THE F-22 RAPTOR. THE PROGRAM OFFICE EXPECTS TO IMPROVE THE DEVELOPER EXPERIENCE AND TO UTILIZE LEAN PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT TECHNIQUES TO INCREASE AND ACCELERATE FEATURE INTEGRATION AND DEPLOYMENT VELOCITY. DEVELOPMENT IN A CLOUD-BASED ENVIRONMENT WILL PROVIDE THE F-22 SPO AN EDGE BY REMOVING BARRIERS TO DELIVER CAPABILITIES TO THE FIGHT. THE F-22 SPO HAS TWO SPECIFIC OBJECTIVE THAT THEY EXPECT TO ACCOMPLISH IN MIGRATING SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT TO A CLOUD COMPUTER SERVICE. FIRST, THE SPO WILL LEVERAGE THE ""BEST IN BREED"" AGILE TOOLS TO DRIVE CAPABILITY FOR THE F-22. SECOND, THE SPO WILL ADOPT CLOUD DOMAINS TO DECREASE DEPENDENCE ON PHYSICAL INFRASTRUCTURE.

prime_award_base_transaction_description: IGF::OT::IGF GROUND SYSTEMS AND MISSION OPERATIONS 2 (GSMO-2).  THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROCUREMENT IS TO ACQUIRE ENGINEERING SERVICES FOR GROUND SYSTEMS AND MISSION OPERATIONS SUPPORT TO MORE THAN 20 MISSIONS IN PHASES OF THE LIFECYCLE FROM PRE-PHASE A TO PHASE E. THE SCOPE ENCOMPASSES CONCEPT STUDIES, DEVELOPMENT (DEFINITION AND IMPLEMENTATION), INTEGRATION, TEST, VERIFICATION, OPERATIONS, SUSTAINMENT OF MISSION OPERATIONS SYSTEMS, SUBSYSTEMS, AND OPERATIONS PROCESSES AND PROCEDURES. SERVICES INVOLVE SYSTEMS ENGINEERING, FACILITY ENGINEERING, LAUNCH AND EARLY ORBIT SUPPORT, FLIGHT OPERATIONS, FLIGHT DYNAMICS SUPPORT, AND SUSTAINING ENGINEERING.
subawardee_business_types: FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION,VETERAN OWNED BUSINESS,LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY,SERVICE DISABLED VETERAN OWNED

UPON THESE CLOUD PLATFORMS PHOENIXÃ¢Â‚¬Â„¢S NICHE SKILLS INCLUDE
THE ARCHITECTURE AND DEVELOPMENT OF BIG DATA ANALYTICS
THAT OPERATE ON MASSIVE VOLUMES OF DATA-AT-REST (BATCH
ANALYTICS) AND ON MASSIVE VOLUMES OF DATA-IN-MOTION
(STREAMING ANALYTICS)."

subaward_description:
4.1	AUTHORIZATION TO OPERATE (ATO) 
4.1.1	CONTINUOUS MONITORING:  THE CONTRACTOR WILL PROVIDE SUBJECT MATTER EXPERT (SME) SUPPORT FOR THE REQUIRED CONTINUOUS MONITORING OF THE ACT3 OPERATING LOCATION 1 ATO IN ACCORDANCE WITH DOD / AF PROCESS AND PROCEDURES DEFINED IN THE APPROVED ATO PACKAGE. CONTINUOUS MONITORING SUPPORT INCLUDES THE RESPONSIBILITY TO DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT THE PLAN OF ACTIONS & MILESTONES (POA&MS) USED TO ASSIST IN IDENTIFYING, ASSESSING, PRIORITIZING, AND MONITORING PROGRESS OF CORRECTIVE EFFORTS FOR SECURITY WEAKNESSES IDENTIFIED WITHIN THE ATO ENVIRONMENT. THE CONTRACTOR SHALL PROVIDE SME SUPPORT TO TRACK AND ADDRESS SECURITY TECHNICAL IMPLEMENTATION GUIDE (STIG) FINDINGS IN SUPPORT OF THE POA&MS TO INCLUDE ROUTINE LOG REVIEW, PATCHING, AND SECURITY SCAN REVIEWS. THE CONTRACTOR WILL ALSO BE RESPONSIBLE FOR RESPONDING TO REQUESTS FROM THE AFRL INFORMATION SYSTEM SECURITY OFFICER AND THE AUTHORIZING OFFICIAL FOR INFORMATION REGARDING THE ATO ENVIRONMENT. 
4.1.2	CHANGE CONTROL BOARD: THE CONTRACTOR WILL PROVIDE SME SUPPORT FOR THE ACT3 ATO ENVIRONMENT CHANGE CONTROL BOARD (CCB), A REQUIRED CHECK AND BALANCE ON CONFIGURATION CHANGE ACTIVITY, ASSURING THAT CHANGES ARE HELD TO ORGANIZATIONALLY DEFINED CRITERIA (E.G., SCOPE, COST, IMPACT ON SECURITY) BEFORE BEING IMPLEMENTED. SME SUPPORT INCLUDES REVIEW OF CHANGE REQUESTS AND RELATED DOCUMENTATION AS WELL AS, COMPLETION OF CHANGE REQUEST SECURITY ASSESSMENTS, INCLUDING SECURITY SCANS, TO MAKE INFORMED RECOMMENDATIONS TO THE ATO ENVIRONMENT CORE OWNER REGARDING APPROVAL, REJECTION, OR DEFERMENT OF CHANGE REQUESTS.
4.1.3	ANNUAL SELF-ASSESSMENT:  THE CONTRACTOR WILL COMPLETE, IN ACCORDANCE WITH DOD / AF PROCESS AND PROCEDURES DEFINED IN THE APPROVED ATO PACKAGE, AN ANNUAL SELF-ASSESSMENT OF THE ACT3 OPERATING LOCATION 1 ENVIRONMENT TO INCLUDE PREPARATION AND SUBMISSION OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTATION TO THE AFRL INFORMATION SYSTEM SECURITY OFFICER AND THE AUTHORIZING OFFICIAL.  
4.2	ACT3 INFRASTRUCTURE AND USER SUPPORT: 
4.2.1	USER SUPPORT:  THE CONTRACTOR WILL PROVIDE SUPPORT TO ACT3 USERS OF THE ACT3 NETWORK. THE CONTACTOR WILL MAINTAIN A TICKETING SYSTEM (ITOP), IN ADDITION TO A SUPPORT EMAIL INBOX, TO RECEIVE USER QUESTIONS, CONCERNS, AND REQUESTS FOR ASSISTANCE. THE USER TICKETS WILL BE TRACKED AND ARCHIVED IN THE TICKETING SYSTEM. SUPPORT REQUESTS COULD INCLUDE REQUEST FOR A NEW USER ACCOUNT, PASSWORD RESETS, FOR CURRENT USERS, REPORTING OF NETWORK PERFORMANCE ISSUES, REQUESTS FOR ASSISTANCE WITH SOFTWARE OR A SERVICE, OR OTHER CONCERNS AS IDENTIFIED BY THE USERS.
4.2.2	HARDWARE SUPPORT:  THE CONTRACTOR WILL MANAGE AND MAINTAIN ALL HARDWARE COMPONENTS OF ACT3 INFRASTRUCTURE BOTH INSIDE THE ATO BOUNDARY AND OUTSIDE OF THE ATO BOUNDARY. THE CONTRACTOR WILL PERFORM ROUTINE HARDWARE MAINTENANCE REQUIRED FOR A STABLE NETWORK. THE CONTRACTOR WILL CONSIDER AND RECOMMEND OPTIONS TO OPTIMIZE THE NETWORK AND INFRASTRUCTURE PERFORMANCE, INCLUDING THE OPTIMIZATION AND DEVELOPMENT OF UNIQUE CONFIGURATION CHANGES / CUSTOM NETWORK ARCHITECTURE SOLUTIONS DESIGNED TO PROTECT INTERNAL RESOURCES AND EMPOWER EXTERNAL DEVELOPMENT AND COLLABORATION. THE CONTRACTOR WILL TROUBLESHOOT ROOT CAUSES OF UNEXPECTED HARDWARE PERFORMANCE AND/OR FAILURE. THE CONTRACTOR WILL COORDINATE SERVICE TICKETS AND SERVICE TECHNICIAN SUPPORT (REMOTE OR ONSITE) AS REQUIRED TO IDENTIFY STEPS TO REMEDIATE UNEXPECTED HARDWARE PERFORMANCE AND/OR FAILURE. 
4.2.3	SOFTWARE AND SERVICES SUPPORT:  THE CONTRACTOR WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR IMPLEMENTATION, SUPPORT, AND MAINTENANCE OF SOFTWARE AND SERVICES INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO:  ANYCONNECT VPN, ANSIBLE,GITLAB/MATTERMOST, ITOP ITSM SOLUTION (USER SUPPORT REQUESTS), ACTIVE DIRECTORY (USER ONBOARDING / OFFBOARDING AND ACCOUNT MANAGEMENT), VM ENVIRONMENTS (DEMONSTRATIONS / SERVICE OPTIMIZATION / ETC.), OWNCLOUD / ONLYOFFICE, ACME DMZ, CERTIFICATE MANAGEMENT, POWER MANAGEMENT, ASSET TRACKING/MANAGEMENT, IP ADDRESS MANAGEMENT, ACE-HUB FEATURE SUPPORT 
4.3	MIDDLE-AGENT SYSTEM MIDDLEWARE (MASM):  THE CONTACTOR WILL SUPPORT THE MASM RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT EFFORTS AS REQUIRED BY THE CUSTOMER, THESE ACTIVITIES INCLUDE CONFIGURING CLOUD TO WORK WITHIN ACE-HUB, CONDUCTING INTERNODE COMMUNICATION TESTING BETWEEN CLOUD NODES, TESTING OF JAVA, C++, AND PYTHON AGENTS. 
4.4	CLOUD ENGINEERING:  THE CONTRACTOR WILL DEPLOY THE LATEST VERSION OF DISA'S BIG DATA PLATFORM (BDP) TO AMAZON WEB SERVICES (AWS) FOR TESTING DEPLOYMENT OF ACE-HUB TO THE BDP. THE CONTRACTOR WILL ALSO PROVIDE SME SUPPORT FOR GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM (GCP) DEPLOYMENT OF ACE-HUB DURING PRE-DEPLOYMENT, DEPLOYMENT, AND MAINTENANCE OF THE ENVIRONMENT, AS NEEDED.  THE CONTRACTOR SHALL DEVELOP AND COORDINATE ANALYTIC CAPABILITIES ON AFRL CLOUD INSTANTIATIONS.

subaward_description: THE NHTSA HAS RECENTLY MODERNIZED ITS CRASH TEST DATABASE AND IS NOW HOSTING IT ON AMAZON WEB SERVICES (AWS). AS PART OF THIS MODERNIZATION, API ENDPOINTS HAVE BEEN DEVELOPED FOR ACCESSING THE DATA. THE OVERARCHING GOAL OF THE NEW CRASH TEST DATABASE SIGNAL ANALYSIS TOOLS (RFP: 693JJ923R000183) PROGRAM IS TO DEVELOP A WEB APPLICATION VERSION OF THE NHTSA VEHICLE SAFETY RESEARCH SIGNAL ANALYSIS TOOL TO MOVE AWAY FROM THE EXISTING WINDOWS-BASED PROGRAM AND TOWARDS A TOOLSET ACCESSIBLE THROUGH A WEB BROWSER. ELEMANCE WILL WORK CLOSELY WITH WAKE FOREST UNIVERSITY HEALTH SCIENCES (WFUHS) TO SUPPORT THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE NEW SIGNAL ANALYSIS TOOL WEB APPLICATION. THE ALLOCATION OF EFFORT FOR EACH TASK IN THE RFP HAS BEEN OUTLINED IN THE FULL PROPOSAL PREPARED BY WFUHS AND ELEMANCE. INITIALLY, ELEMANCE WILL ASSIST WFUHS IN DEVELOPING A TASK IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (C.5.2). THE GOAL OF THE TASK IMPLEMENTATION PLAN IS TO DETAIL THE SCHEDULE, TECHNICAL APPROACH, CODE REVIEW, AND DOCUMENTATION STRATEGY FOR THE SIGNAL ANALYSIS TOOL. ONCE THE DRAFT PLAN HAS BEEN APPROVED BY NHTSA, THE PROJECT TEAM WILL PROCEED WITH TOOL DEVELOPMENT (C.5.3). THE TEAM OF ENGINEERS AT ELEMANCE DEDICATED TO SUPPORTING THIS PROGRAM HAVE EXPERIENCE IN EACH COMPONENT OF THE DESIGN PROCESS REQUIRED TO MAKE THE SIGNAL ANALYSIS WEB APPLICATION A SUCCESS, INCLUDING FRONT END USER INTERFACE DEVELOPMENT, DATABASE AND API ABSTRACTION, FUNCTIONAL AND OBJECT-ORIENTED PROGRAMMING FOR SIGNAL ANALYSIS, AND EXPERIENCE IN CALCULATING AND VISUALIZING THE METRICS REQUIRED BY THE DELIVERED TOOL. ELEMANCE WILL ASSIST WFUHS IN THE DEVELOPMENT OF FRONT-END INTERFACES FOR INTERACTING WITH AND VISUALIZING SIGNAL DATA, BACK-END INFRASTRUCTURE FOR INTERACTING WITH THE DATABASE APIS, AND FUNCTIONAL PROGRAMMING FOR CALCULATIONS PERFORMED ON SIGNAL DATA OBTAINED FROM THE DATABASE. IN THE EVENT THAT OPTIONAL TASK C.5.4 IS EXECUTED, ELEMANCE WILL ALSO SUPPORT WFUHS ENGINEERS IN THE EXPANSION OF THE SIGNAL ANALYSIS TOOLS TO INCLUDE THE FEATURES OUTLINED IN THE RFP. ELEMANCE WILL SUPPORT THIS EFFORT BY PROVIDING OBJECTORIENTED PROGRAMMING AND CONTRIBUTING TO THE FUNCTIONAL PROGRAMMING REQUIRED TO INTEGRATE THE ADDITIONAL FEATURES REQUESTED BY NHTSA. ELEMANCE HAS ALSO PUT TOGETHER A TEAM OF ENGINEERS CAPABLE OF SUPPORTING AN ADDITIONAL OPTIONAL TASK (C.5.5) THAT, IF EXECUTED, WILL FOCUS ON DEVELOPING ADDITIONAL FEATURES DESIRED OR REQUESTED BY NHTSA. WHILE NO DELIVERABLES FOR THIS OPTIONAL TASK ARE OUTLINED IN THE RFP, THE ALLOCATION OF EFFORT FOR THE ELEMANCE PROJECT TEAM HAS BEEN COMMITTED WITH PERCENTAGES COMMENSURATE WITH THE OTHER TASKS WITHIN THE RFP
